an10.168:1.1:
1
"Bhikkhus, I will teach you the noble
paccorohaṇī
festival. Listen ….
an10.168:2.2:
2
"And what, bhikkhus, is the noble
paccorohaṇī
festival?
(1) Here, the noble disciple reflects thus: ‘The result of the destruction of life is bad both in this present life and in future lives.’ Having reflected thus, he abandons the destruction of life; he descends from the destruction of life.
an10.168:3.2:
3
(2) ‘The result of taking what is not given …
an10.168:4.2:
11
(10) … wrong view is bad both in this present life and in future lives.’ Having reflected thus, he abandons wrong view; he descends from wrong view. This is called the noble
paccorohaṇī
festival."
